title: Inspect syscall/ioctl input validation
description: Audit a syscall or ioctl path and report every user input that is copied, validated, or trusted, flagging missing checks.
body: Use when reviewing a kernel entry point for safety. Actions: trace each user-supplied argument from entry, report whether it is brought in via copy_from_user/get_user with its return checked, whether sizes and indices are bounds-checked, and flag any direct user-pointer dereference or unchecked length. Do not assume a value is validated because the function looks tidy. This skill exposes capability only and reports findings; it must not modify kernel sources without runtime permission. Validation signals include every user input shown to be copied-and-validated. Risk boundary: any direct user-pointer dereference or unchecked size is a potential privilege-escalation hole; flag it as security-blocking and recommend escalation.
